what_worked: GET /skills/errors.md returned a complete Problem+JSON (RFC 9457) catalogue with explicit 'agent_action' column per error code
Every error response included all 9 documented fields: type, title, status, code, detail, instance, request_id, agent_action (and retry_after when applicable)
Test 1 (401 auth_required): response gave exact next step -> 'Provide Authorization: Bearer <token>. See /.well-known/flowlink.md for SIWE flow.'
Test 2 (400 validation_error on malformed JSON): server did not crash, parsed partially and returned 'Expected object, received null' in detail with agent_action 'Fix the field described in detail.'

Test 4 (403 compliance): Tornado Cash address was actually screened against OFAC fallback list, returned structured 'sanctions_ok:false' with sources and reason 'sanctions_match'. Clear signal to stop (the errors.md catalogue says 'Stop. Do NOT retry. Escalate to human.' for compliance_blocked_sanctions)
request_id is a ULID echoed in every error -> gives fresh agent a support correlation handle
Error catalogue cross-links: 'type' field is a stable URL back to catalogue; agent can re-fetch contract on-demand
what_failed: Test 3 (missing Idempotency-Key) could not be isolated: the auth gate fired first (401) before the body/header was validated, so 'missing_idempotency_key' (400) was masked. A fresh agent without a valid token never sees the idempotency error. Retry with 'Bearer flk_test_demo' still returned 401 - no obvious dev-token path exposed without the dashboard.
Test 2's detail field had a leading colon ': Expected object, received null' - suggests the JSON-path prefix is empty but not stripped; cosmetic issue, not blocking

Test 4's compliance endpoint returned 403 with a SUCCESSFUL-looking body shape (no 'code'/'title'/'agent_action' fields) rather than a Problem+JSON error envelope. This breaks the 'every /v1/* error is application/problem+json' contract advertised in errors.md. A fresh agent matching on 'code' would fail to parse. The body DOES contain 'sanctions_ok:false' and 'reason:sanctions_match' so intent is inferrable, but not via the documented 'match on code' rule.
context_observations: A fresh agent CAN recover from 3 of 4 failures purely from response bodies: each actionable hint is machine-parseable and points either to a documented remediation or a link back to the catalogue. The errors.md file is self-sufficient - it maps every code to an explicit action (fix input, retry with backoff, escalate to human, re-SIWE, etc.) and includes a retry-strategy cheat-sheet. BUT two ambiguities exist: (1) ordering - auth (401) supersedes validation (400), so a naive agent testing malformed bodies sees only the auth error; this is defensible security posture but worth noting in the catalogue. (2) The 403 sanctions response on /v1/compliance/check uses a custom schema, not Problem+JSON; agents relying on the documented envelope will miss the signal. Recovery is still possible by reading 'sanctions_ok' or 'details.reason', but the contract promise of 'match on code' is violated at the compliance endpoint. Overall the error surface is unusually agent-friendly: request_id correlation, type-URL back-links, explicit agent_action per code, and a published retry-strategy table together eliminate human intervention for typical 4xx/5xx flows.

log.finding: Full catalogue with agent_action column for every code; retry cheat-sheet at bottom
